2604.24519.title: Why AI Harms Can't Be Fixed One Identity at a Time: What 5300 Incident Reports Reveal About Intersectionality
2604.24519.paper_summary: AI risk assessment is the primary tool for identifying harms caused by AI systems. These include intersectional harms, which arise from the interaction between identity categories (e.g., class and skin tone) and which do not occur, or occur differently, when those categories are considered separately. Yet existing AI risk assessments are still built around isolated identity categories, and when intersections are considered, they focus almost exclusively on race and gender. Drawing on a large-scale analysis of documented AI incidents, we show that AI harms do not occur one identity category at a time. Using a structured rubric applied with a Large Language Model (LLM), we analyze 5,300 reports from 1,200 documented incidents in the AI Incident Database, the most curated source of incident data. From these reports, we identify 1,513 harmed subjects and their associated identity categories, achieving 98% accuracy. At the level of individual categories, we find that age and political identity appear in documented AI harms at rates comparable to race and gender. At the level of intersecting categories, harm is amplified up to three times at specific intersections: adolescent girls, lower-class people of color, and upper-class political elites. We argue that intersectionality should be a core component of AI risk assessment to more accurately capture how harms are produced and distributed across social groups.
2604.24519.paper_summary_zh: AI 風險評估是識別 AI 系統造成的傷害的主要工具。這些傷害包括交叉傷害，這些傷害源於身份類別之間的互動（例如，階級和膚色），而當這些類別分開考慮時，這些傷害不會發生或以不同方式發生。然而，現有的 AI 風險評估仍然圍繞孤立的身份類別構建，當考慮交叉時，它們幾乎專注於種族和性別。根據對記錄的 AI 事件的大規模分析，我們顯示 AI 傷害並不是一次發生在一個身份類別上。使用結構化的評分標準，並應用於大型語言模型 (LLM)，我們分析了來自 AI 事件數據庫的 5,300 份報告，這是最經過策劃的事件數據來源。從這些報告中，我們識別出 1,513 名受害者及其相關的身份類別，達到 98% 的準確率。在個別類別層面，我們發現年齡和政治身份在記錄的 AI 傷害中出現的比率與種族和性別相當。在交叉類別層面，特定交叉點的傷害增強了多達三倍：青少年女孩、低階層有色人種和上層階級政治精英。我們認為，交叉性應該是 AI 風險評估的核心組成部分，以更準確地捕捉傷害是如何在社會群體中產生和分配的。
2604.24519.author: Edyta Bogucka et.al.
2604.24519.authors: Edyta Bogucka, Sanja Šćepanović, Daniele Quercia
